Based on the context provided, **I cannot answer that question**. The memos do confirm that code enforcement cases have been rising — from 73 cases (late February/early March) up to a 2026 high of 125 cases (week of April 17–23) — so the upward trend is clearly documented.

However, the context contains **no information about a "new citation policy"** or any explanation for *why* cases have been increasing. The memos are routine weekly snapshots that report numbers only, without analysis of causes or references to any policy changes.

To answer whether a new citation policy is responsible, you would need to consult additional sources beyond what is provided here.
